extraction.one_line_summary: HODEG won stage 3 after MØRKØV executed a surprise early leadout that caught ACKERMANN out of position on the inside.
tactical_patterns: MØRKØV's deliberately early leadout from behind caught ACKERMANN committed to wrong wheels, revealing surprise timing beats pure speed.
ACKERMANN's pattern of diving inside through traffic backfired when trapped without exit, exposing predictable positioning weakness.
Quick-Step isolated ACKERMANN by attacking before Lotto-Jumbo's train formed, showing sprinter separation requires disrupting opponent's setup phase.

rider_signals.HODEG Álvaro José: positive: first win after MØRKØV perfected early surprise launch, proving Quick-Step finally calibrated timing to neutralize ACKERMANN
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: third stage attempt failed when predictable inside moves left him boxed without positioning options at 300m
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: positive: Danish champion's tactical early acceleration from behind created surprise element that dominant sprinters couldn't counter
rider_signals.SABATINI Fabio: positive: initiated Quick-Step's winning sequence with well-timed early move that set up MØRKØV's surprise launch
